chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُدَلِّسُ نَفْسَهُ وَ الْعِنِّينِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 67 – The man who cheats and the impotent

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/160)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/160)

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ خَصِيّاً دَلَّسَ نَفْسَهُ لِامْرَأَةٍ

text: يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا وَ تَأْخُذُ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْهُ صَدَاقَهَا وَ يُوجَعُ ظَهْرُهُ كَمَا دَلَّسَ نَفْسَهُ.

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from his brother Al Hassan, from Zur’at Bin Muhammad, from Sama’at,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> if a eunuch deceived the woman. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Separation would be effected between the two, and the woman would take her dower from him, and his back would be pained just as he had deceived himself’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Sa‘id from his brother al-Hassan ibn Zur’ah ibn Muhammad ibn Sanaa ‘ah who has said the following: “About the case of a man who is castrated but he cheats and marries a woman, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘They are separated from each other and the women takes her mahr (dower) from him and he will have a backache (punishment) because of his cheating.’”
